topic: A person's car is towed from their own driveway
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence of the empty driveway was deafening. Elias stood by the curb, his hand gripping the cold metal of the railing, his knuckles white. He kept glancing toward the street, expecting to see the familiar silhouette of his sedan, but there was only an unmarked tow truck idling nearby. A slow, heavy dread settled in his chest, making each breath shallow. He tried to rationalize it—a misplaced tag, a misunderstanding—but the sheer absurdity of the situation refused to settle into logic. He took a hesitant step back, his gaze darting from the truck to the vacant space where his car should have been, a tremor running through his hands.

[story 2]

"No. No, that can't be right." Sarah whispered, her voice thin and reedy against the morning air. She paced three steps toward the house, then stopped abruptly, spinning around to face the empty spot in the gravel. Her jaw tightened, a muscle jumping near her temple. She ran a hand through her hair, pulling at the strands as if to physically smooth away the sudden disarray inside her. The sunlight felt too bright, exposing the stark reality of the situation. She just stood there, rooted, unable to move forward or back.

[story 3]

I watched the tow truck pull away, its engine a harsh, grating sound that seemed to amplify the hollowness in my stomach. My feet felt strangely disconnected from the ground, as if gravity had suddenly decided to pull me sideways. I walked slowly toward the front door, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. When I reached the threshold, I leaned against the frame, closing my eyes tightly. A wave of nausea washed over me, and I pressed the heels of my palms against my eyes, trying to physically block out the sight of the empty space where my vehicle had rested moments before.
